resources.string: Intercepts and remaps BYD steering wheel hardware buttons to custom actions and third-party apps.
🟡 Enabled — waiting for OS to start…
⚠️ Accessibility Service is not running. Key interception will not work.
Tip: If your buttons aren\'t listed, use the menu (top right) -> \'Debug Steering Wheel\' to find your specific keycodes. Note: Short and Long presses often send different keycodes.
Project a view or an entire application onto the secondary instrument cluster display.

Simulates clicking the system split-screen button for incompatible systems.

Requirements:
1. The split-screen button must be on your system bar (e.g., position 4/5, left of the center icon).
2. Configure the X/Y coordinates below to match your screen resolution.
It is for systems with UI 7 in which the built-in split-screen mode no longer allows any app, but only built-in ones. So we use a custom solution to cast apps in the main screen.
Natively patches installed applications by modifying their APK. This allows you to split-screen any app on DiLink 6 (UI 7).
These actions run automatically each time the car connects to the app.
No startup actions yet. Tap "Add Action" to begin.
No logs loaded. Click "Execute logcat" to retrieve logs.
Adds dark edge gradients at the top and bottom of the projected image to improve readability of the cars physical white instrument lettering.
Adds an additional contrast gradient to the left side of the projection.

How it works:
Casting apps to the secondary dashboard/instrument cluster display can overlay white backgrounds on top of the car's physical white speedometer/tachometer indicators, making them hard to read. Enabling this feature overlays subtle, semi-transparent gradient bars (black for Dark Mode, white for Light Mode) at the top and bottom edges of the projected area to keep your dashboard readable. You can choose whether these gradients automatically follow your theme, or manually force them.

Options:
• Contrast Edge Gradients Toggle: Enables or disables the protective contrast gradients at the screen edges.

How it works:
This menu is designed to configure how an app should be cast to your car's secondary instrument cluster display screen. Once configured, it is recommended to bind the launch action on the dashboard or toggle cluster projection to a physical steering wheel button, and interact with your apps in this way.

Options:
• App to Cast: Search and select any installed app to cast. The settings below will be saved specifically for this application; if no parameters are configured for the app, default settings will be used.
• Cluster Layout Mode:
 - Auto-detect from car: Automatically matches the display layout based on vehicle status. (Note: This may not work on UI 7 systems).
 - Force Mini Mode: Preferred mode as it still allows the ADAS system/view to be visible.
 - Force Fullscreen Mode: Scales the projected app to fit the entire screen.
• Custom Layout Settings (Fullscreen / Mini):
 - Width / Height: Adjust the exact width and height of the projection in pixels.
 - X / Y Offset: Move the projection horizontally (X) or vertically (Y) to position it perfectly on your screen.
 - Density DPI: Adjust the rendering scale (zoom in/out) of the projected application.
• Cast & Save: Starts projection immediately and saves your layout configurations.
• Stop Projection: Immediately closes the active projection on your cluster screen.

💡 TROUBLESHOOTING TIP: If casting to the dashboard is not working, check the System Diagnostics view. Confirm if the app is picking the correct display and run 'Test Overlay' on the target screen until it displays successfully.

How it works:\nThis menu is designed to configure how an app should be cast to your car\'s secondary instrument cluster display screen. You can select an app, choose its layout mode, configure custom dimensions/offsets, and add automated actions (simulated taps) that run when casting. Once configured, you can launch or toggle projection using steering wheel button mappings.

How it works:
Defines how the app's projection display layout should be structured on the cluster screen.

Modes:
• Auto-detect from car: Automatically matches the layout based on vehicle status.
• Force Mini Mode: Preferred mode as it still allows the ADAS system/view to be visible.
• Force Fullscreen Mode: Scales the projected app to fit the entire cluster display.

How it works:
Allows you to override the default dimensions, offsets, and density (DPI) for rendering on the secondary screen.

Adjustments:
• Width / Height: Adjusts the rendering area resolution in pixels.
• X / Y Offset: Positions the projected window on the physical cluster screen (aligns positive/negative coordinates).
• Density DPI: Adjusts the rendering scale (zoom in/out) of the projected application. Lower values fit more content, higher values enlarge UI elements.

How it works:
App Automations are simulated screen taps executed automatically after the app is casted. This is useful for automatically dismissing warning prompts, clicking login buttons, or switching views.

Configuration:
• Delay: Amount of milliseconds to wait after the casting starts before simulating the tap.
• Coordinates (X, Y): Percentage-based position on the screen. (0%, 0%) represents the top-left corner, and (100%, 100%) represents the bottom-right corner.

How it works:
Allows you to reprogram the physical hardware buttons on your steering wheel to perform custom actions (like opening split-screen apps, adjusting the air conditioning, or toggling projection).

Actions & Configuration:
• Assign Action: Select the action to trigger:
 - Launch App: Opens a chosen application on the main display.
 - Launch App on Cluster: Opens the chosen application on the instrument cluster display.
 - Split Screen Apps: Launches two applications side-by-side.
 * Under Projected split screen mode, you can configure:
 - Projected Target: Pick whether the first or second app is rendered inside the custom projection overlay.
 - Split Ratio: Choose how much space each app occupies on the screen (1/3, 1/2, or 2/3).
 - Toggle AC: Instantly turns AC power on/off.
 - Toggle Driver Window: Rolls the driver's front window up or down.
 - Toggle Cluster Projection: Sends the current app in the dashboard back to the main screen. If there is no app in the dashboard, it takes whatever app is currently open on the main screen and projects it to the cluster. Clicking it again pulls the app back to the main screen. (Note: Using 'Launch App on Cluster' is highly recommended instead, as it is less prone to errors because it targets a specific app).

How it works:
This section is for testing that the car-app integration works as expected. It allows manual control of windows and climate settings to verify that connectivity and commands are functioning properly.

Options:
• Driver Front Window / All Windows: Roll down (open) or roll up (close) specific windows or all windows at once.
• Climate Control (AC): Turn the vehicle's air conditioning on or off to test the connection.

How it works:
Defines a list of automated actions that execute one after another in a specified sequence when the car starts.

DISCLAIMER: For this to work properly, you must go to the car's settings app, navigate to self-start settings, disable self-start, and uncheck this app to allow it to self-start.

Options:
• Delay before action: Pause for up to 15 seconds before launching this step. NOTE: If multiple apps are opened at the same time, the system UI may become unstable or buggy. If this happens, add a short delay between launching apps until the sequence executes stably.
• Add Action: Creates a new automated step in the sequence.
• Test Run: Executes the entire sequence step-by-step in real-time, helping you test and fine-tune your delays.

How it works:
Defines how the app coordinates split-screen modes, allowing full compatibility across different generations of the car's system UI.

Options:
• Native Mode: Standard system split-screen. Recommended for systems that fully support native third-party app splitting.
• Native UI 7 (DiLink 6): Natively patches installed applications by modifying their APK. This allows you to split-screen any app on DiLink 6 (UI 7).
• Projected Mode: Custom split layout that overlays one app on top of another. Bypasses strict UI 7 split-screen restrictions and allows custom ratio adjustments.
• Native (Simulated Tap): Bypasses split restrictions on certain firmware versions by simulating a precise screen touch/tap on the system's native split-screen toolbar button.
Casting not working? Check if the app is picking the correct display. Click to open Diagnostics, scroll to Recognized Screens, and test overlays until one works on your dashboard.

These actions run automatically when the application is cast to the cluster display, allowing simulated taps at specific coordinates. X controls the horizontal position (0% left to 100% right), and Y controls the vertical position (0% top to 100% bottom), where (0%, 0%) represents the top-left corner.
No automated tasks yet. Tap \"Add Task\" to begin.

This setting determines where the first application launches:

• If NO split screen is currently active: The first app will open on the Left side, and the second app will open on the Right side.
• If a split screen is ALREADY open: The first app will launch in the specific target size/pane selected (1/3 or 2/3).

The proxy is a vital part of the application. If it is not running, most features and the application itself will not work. We advise checking if USB/ADB debugging is enabled and authorized in your developer options, restarting the app or the car, and ensuring you grant ADB access to this application.

The SDK is a minor component that could be ignored, not all vehicles are compatible. However, direct vehicle access and commands (such as rolling down the windows) as well as some auto detection features will not function without it.
This service handles steering wheel key interception and button remapping. It is most likely to run and work properly if the main ADB proxy is up and connected.

These configurations are applied within this application. However, you must also change the corresponding setting inside the official BYD map app to match the chosen mode (Mini or Fullscreen).\n\nThis is especially important for DiLink 5 systems; on DiLink 6 it does not matter.
